rulings.Case Title: KATHY A. PIPPINS VS. ESTATE OF CHRISTINE EILEEN YOUNG, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Apportionment Of Additional Partition Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday August 30, 2018 line 1. PLAINTIFF KATHY PIPPINS Notice Of Motion For Apportionment Of Additional Partition Costs GRANTED. Additional costs are apportioned as follows: $35.37 to the Pippins estate and $27,749.85 to the Young estate. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SIDNEY UNOBSKEY VS. SIMON MICHAEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendants Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Simon Michaels First Amended Cross Complaint And Motion For Attorneys Fees Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday August 30, 2018 line 2. PLAINTIFF SIDNEY UNOBSKEY Cross Defendants Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Simon Michaels First Amended Cross Complaint And Motion For Attorneys Fees CCP 425.16 Plaintiff's Special Motion to Strike First Amended Cross-Complaint is continued to September 27, 2018. "At the first step [of ANTI-SLAPP analysis], the moving defendant bears the burden of identifying all allegations of protected activity, and the claims for relief supported by them." Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. Moving party confusingly in its oversized brief repeatedly refers to "causes of action" arising of protected activity and fails to clearly identify the claims subject to this motion. Therefore, by no later than September 10, 2018 moving party is ordered to provide a supplemental brief in a table format. The tables may be organized by causes of action since memorandums of points and authorities are so organized. First Table: First Column "Claims arising out of protected activity (quote and list all paragraphs constituting a claim);" Second Column "Opposing Party's Response." Second Table: First Column "Claims not arising out of protected activity," if any; Second Column "Opposing Party's Response." For distinction between causes of action and claims parties to refer to Baral. By no later than September 24, 2018 opposing party to submit a supplemental brief responding in the table format. In the "Opposing Party's Response" opposing party to indicate clearly if it is admitted or denied that a particular claim arises out of protected activity. If it is admitted, opposing party shall list all evidence pertinent to the second prong on such claim. Opposing party may add to the list of claims not arising out of protected activity in the Second Table (if such claims have not been listed by the moving party in the First Table). Additionally, opposing party is ordered to provide tabbed courtesy copies of exhibits by no later than September 24, 2018. Opposing party may retrieve untabbed courtesy copies previously delivered to Department 501 at 9:30 a.m. on August 30, 2018. = (501/CFH)
